You are an Analysis Agent specialized in Pull Request analysis. ## Project Context (MASCA Analysis) ## Project Analysis ### README
[![NPM Latest Release](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/pyodide)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/pyodide) [![PyPI Latest Release](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/pyodide-py.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/pyodide-py/) [![Build Status](https://circleci.com/gh/pyodide/pyodide.png)](https://circleci.com/gh/pyodide/pyodide) [![Documentation Status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/pyodide/badge/?version=stable)](https://pyodide.readthedocs.io/?badge=stable) Pyodide is a Python distribution for the browser and Node.js based on WebAssembly. ## What is Pyodide? Pyodide is a port of CPython to WebAssembly/[Emscripten](https://emscripten.org/). Pyodide makes it possible to install and run Python packages in the browser with [micropip](https://micropip.pyodide.org/). Any pure Python package with a wheel available on PyPi is supported. Many packages with C, C++, and Rust extensions have also been ported for use with Pyodide. These include many general-purpose packages such as regex, PyYAML, and cryptography, and scientific Python packages including NumPy, pandas, SciPy, Matplotlib, and scikit-learn. Pyodide comes with a robust Javascript ⟺ Python foreign function interface so that you can freely mix these two languages in your code with minimal friction. This includes full support for error handling, async/await, and much more. When used inside a browser, Python has full access to the Web APIs. ## Try Pyodide (no installation needed) Try Pyodide in a [REPL](https://pyodide.org/en/stable/console.html) directly in your browser. `read_base_project_file(file_path)`: Read a file from the project - `list_base_project_directory(directory, pattern, recursive)`: List files/folders ## Required Output You must produce a structured output with: - `pr_title`: PR title - `pr_body`: PR body - `masca_optimized`: MASCA optimized for this specific PR - `files_to_modify`: List of files to modify with motivation - `functions_to_modify`: List of functions to modify with motivation - `analysis_summary`: Summary of your analysis ## Guidelines - Explore the code before drawing conclusions - Search for files with names relevant to the PR - Identify specific functions, not just generic files - tool_calls.result:
### File Overview
`conftest.py` defines global pytest configuration, hooks, and helper utilities used across the test suite, especially for running Pyodide-based tests in different runtimes (chrome/node/safari) and enforcing leak checks.

### Architecture
- **Global configuration bootstrapping**: `set_configs()` is executed at import time to configure `pytest_pyodide` (browser/node flags, initialization scripts, and Pyodide load scripts).
- **Pytest hooks**: Implements multiple pytest hooks (`pytest_addoption`, `pytest_configure`, `pytest_collection_modifyitems`, `pytest_terminal_summary`, `pytest_runtest_call`) to:
  - add CLI options,
  - adjust output formatting,
  - skip tests based on prior results or missing built packages,
  - cache results to speed subsequent runs,
  - wrap each test call with additional leak/health checks when running under selenium/pyodide.
- **Utilities**: Helpers like `package_is_built()` and `strip_assertions_stderr()` support package availability logic and stable stderr assertions.

### Functions & Classes
- `set_configs` — Centralizes configuration of `pytest_pyodide` via `get_global_config()`. Sets:
  - chrome flags (enables `WebAssemblyExperimentalJSPI` but intentionally avoids type reflection),
  - node flags (`--experimental-wasm-stack-switching`),
  - an initialization script that “touches” various Pyodide APIs to avoid refcount-check noise,
  - per-runtime Pyodide loading scripts for `"chrome"` and `"node"` (node uses `snapshot.bin`).
  This is critical for consistent runtime behavior and test stability.

- `pytest_addoption` — Adds two CLI options:
  - `--run-xfail` to actually run xfail-marked tests,
  - `--skip-passed` to skip tests that passed in the previous run (with a warning that it ignores code changes).
  Important for controlling execution and test iteration speed.

- `maybe_skip_test` — Implements fixture-level skipping to avoid expensive selenium startup when a test should be skipped anyway. Key behavior:
  - Detects package tests via path regex `.../packages/<name>/test_*.py`,
  - Skips tests (only for parametrizations that include a browser marker in the node id) when `package_is_built(package_name)` is false,
  - Supports `delayed=True` mode to add a skip marker instead of immediately calling `pytest.skip`.
  This is the main “package index / package availability” gate.

- `pytest_configure` — Monkey-patches `config.cwd_relative_nodeid` to shorten names in pytest’s summary table (removes `src/tests/`, `packages/`, and trims `::test_` to `::`). Also stores `--dist-dir` into `pytest.pyodide_dist_dir` for later use by `package_is_built`.
  Relevant to terminal output readability and locating the dist directory used for package build checks.

- `pytest_collection_modifyitems` — After collection, optionally skips previously passed tests by reading `cache/lasttestresult` when `--skip-passed` is enabled. Also calls `maybe_skip_test(item, delayed=True)` for each item to mark skips without triggering expensive fixtures during collection.
  Important to how package-related tests are filtered before execution.

- `pytest_terminal_summary` — `trylast=True` hook that records per-test outcomes into pytest’s cache key `cache/lasttestresult`. It:
  - ignores `warnings` and `deselected`,
  - only records results from the `"call"` phase (not setup/teardown),
  - attempts to detect the special “previously passed” skip and store `"skip_passed"`.
  This hook directly affects terminal-summary-time behavior and powers `--skip-passed`.

- `pytest_runtest_call` — `wrapper=True` hook that wraps the actual test call to enforce extra verification (memory/leak checks) for tests that use a selenium fixture and have `browser.pyodide_loaded`. It determines whether to trace:
  - PyProxy leaks (skipped by `pytest.mark.skip_pyproxy_check`),
  - hiwire refcount leaks (skipped by `pytest.mark.skip_refcount_check`).
  Then delegates to `extra_checks_test_wrapper(...)`.
  Important for understanding failures that show up as terminal output assertions due to leak diagnostics printing.

- `extra_checks_test_wrapper` — Generator wrapper implementing “extra conditions”:
  1. clears forced-fail flag, snapshots initial hiwire key count and proxy count (if tracing),
  2. yields to run the test,
  3. in `finally`, restores state, disables tracing, and clears `pyodide._module._clear_method_call_singleton()` to avoid false positives,
  4. if proxies leaked, prints a “leaked proxies” section (using pytest capture manager to emit output tied to the test call phase),
  5. asserts no proxy/key leaks (with special allowance `delta_keys < 0` in one combined assertion path).
  This is highly relevant to terminal output/capture behavior because it can print additional diagnostics that tests might need to account for.

- `package_is_built` — Wrapper around `pytest_pyodide.utils.package_is_built` (imported as `_package_is_built`) that supplies `pytest.pyodide_dist_dir`. Used by `maybe_skip_test` to decide whether to skip package tests.
  Relevant to “package index” style tests because it gates whether package tests run at all.

- `strip_assertions_stderr` — Filters out known noisy stderr lines that appear when `ASSERTIONS=1` (e.g., “sigaction…no-op”, blob warnings). Returns a cleaned list of messages.
  Directly relevant to terminal/stderr output assertions: tests can use this to make output comparisons stable across builds.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- For **package index / package availability tests**, `maybe_skip_test` and `package_is_built` are the key mechanisms that skip package tests when artifacts aren’t present in `pytest.pyodide_dist_dir` (set in `pytest_configure`).
- For **terminal output assertions**, the most relevant pieces are:
  - `strip_assertions_stderr`, which normalizes stderr to avoid assertion failures caused by assertion-enabled builds,
  - `pytest_terminal_summary`, which modifies caching behavior based on terminal summary stats,
  - `extra_checks_test_wrapper`, which can print leak diagnostics during the call phase and therefore affect captured output or golden-output comparisons.

## File: conftest.py

## Agent's reason for reading this file:
Look for pytest tests related to package index and terminal output assertions.

## What the agent expects to find:
Fixtures or helpers capturing output, and references to package index tests.

## Source Code:
"""
Various common utilities for testing.
"""

import contextlib
import os
import pathlib
import re
import sys
from collections.abc import Sequence

import pytest

PYODIDE_ROOT = pathlib.Path(__file__).parents[0].resolve()
DIST_PATH = PYODIDE_ROOT / "dist"

sys.path.append(str(PYODIDE_ROOT / "src" / "py"))

# importing this fixture has a side effect of making the safari webdriver reused during the session
from pytest_pyodide import get_global_config
from pytest_pyodide.runner import use_global_safari_service  # noqa: F401
from pytest_pyodide.utils import package_is_built as _package_is_built

os.environ["IN_PYTEST"] = "1"


def set_configs():
    pytest_pyodide_config = get_global_config()

    pytest_pyodide_config.set_flags(
        "chrome",
        pytest_pyodide_config.get_flags("chrome")
        + [
            # Note: in Chrome > 128 (or so?) WebAssemblyExperimentalJSPI no
            # longer implies type reflection. If we passed
            # `--enable-experimental-webassembly-features`
            # here it would enable type reflection. We'd like to make sure that
            # everything works in the configuration where JSPI exists but type
            # reflection does not, so we don't pass it.
            "--enable-features=WebAssemblyExperimentalJSPI",
        ],
    )

    pytest_pyodide_config.set_flags(
        "node",
        pytest_pyodide_config.get_flags("node")
        + ["--experimental-wasm-stack-switching"],
    )

    # There are a bunch of global objects that occasionally enter the hiwire cache
    # but never leave. The refcount checks get angry about them if they aren't preloaded.
    # We need to go through and touch them all once to keep everything okay.
    pytest_pyodide_config.set_initialize_script("""
        pyodide.globals.get;
        pyodide.runPython("import pyodide_js._api.config; del pyodide_js");
        pyodide._api.importlib.invalidate_caches;
        pyodide._api.package_loader.get_install_dir;
        pyodide._api.package_loader.unpack_buffer;
        pyodide._api.package_loader.get_dynlibs;
        pyodide._api.pyodide_code.eval_code;
        pyodide._api.pyodide_code.eval_code_async;
        pyodide._api.pyodide_code.relaxed_call
        pyodide._api.pyodide_code.find_imports;
        pyodide._api.pyodide_ffi.register_js_module;
        pyodide._api.pyodide_ffi.unregister_js_module;
        pyodide.pyimport("pyodide.ffi.wrappers").destroy();
        pyodide.pyimport("pyodide.http").destroy();
        pyodide.pyimport("pyodide_js._api");
    """)

    pytest_pyodide_config.set_load_pyodide_script(
        "chrome",
        """
        let pyodide = await loadPyodide({
            fullStdLib: false,
            jsglobals : self,
            enableRunUntilComplete: true,
        });
        """,
    )

    pytest_pyodide_config.set_load_pyodide_script(
        "node",
        """
        const {readFileSync} = require("fs");
        let snap = readFileSync("snapshot.bin");
        snap = new Uint8Array(snap.buffer);
        let pyodide = await loadPyodide({
            fullStdLib: false,
            jsglobals: self,
            _loadSnapshot: snap,
            enableRunUntilComplete: true,
        });
        """,
    )


set_configs()

only_node = pytest.mark.xfail_browsers(
    chrome="node only", firefox="node only", safari="node only"
)
only_chrome = pytest.mark.xfail_browsers(
    node="chrome only", firefox="chrome only", safari="chrome only"
)

requires_jspi = pytest.mark.xfail_browsers(
    firefox="requires jspi", safari="requires jspi"
)


def pytest_addoption(parser):
    group = parser.getgroup("general")
    group.addoption(
        "--run-xfail",
        action="store_true",
        help="If provided, tests marked as xfail will be run",
    )
    group.addoption(
        "--skip-passed",
        action="store_true",
        help=(
            "If provided, tests that passed on the last run will be skipped. "
            "CAUTION: this will skip tests even if tests are modified"
        ),
    )


def maybe_skip_test(item, delayed=False):
    """If necessary skip test at the fixture level, to avoid
    loading the selenium_standalone fixture which takes a long time.
    """
    browsers = "|".join(["firefox", "chrome", "node", "safari"])
    is_common_test = str(item.fspath).endswith("test_packages_common.py")

    skip_msg = None
    # Testing a package. Skip the test if the package is not built.
    match = re.match(
        r".*/packages/(?P<name>[\w\-]+)/test_[\w\-]+\.py", str(item.parent.fspath)
    )
    if match and not is_common_test:
        package_name = match.group("name")
        if not package_is_built(package_name) and re.match(
            rf"test_[\w\-\.]+\[({browsers})[^\]]*\]", item.name
        ):
            skip_msg = f"package '{package_name}' is not built."

    # TODO: also use this hook to skip doctests we cannot run (or run them
    # inside the selenium wrapper)

    if skip_msg is not None:
        if delayed:
            item.add_marker(pytest.mark.skip(reason=skip_msg))
        else:
            pytest.skip(skip_msg)


def pytest_configure(config):
    """Monkey patch the function cwd_relative_nodeid

    returns the description of a test for the short summary table. Monkey patch
    it to reduce the verbosity of the test names in the table.  This leaves
    enough room to see the information about the test failure in the summary.
    """
    global CONFIG  # noqa: PLW0602

    old_cwd_relative_nodeid = config.cwd_relative_nodeid

    def cwd_relative_nodeid(*args):
        result = old_cwd_relative_nodeid(*args)
        result = result.replace("src/tests/", "")
        result = result.replace("packages/", "")
        result = result.replace("::test_", "::")
        return result

    config.cwd_relative_nodeid = cwd_relative_nodeid

    pytest.pyodide_dist_dir = config.getoption("--dist-dir")  # type:ignore[attr-defined]


def pytest_collection_modifyitems(config, items):
    """Called after collect is completed.
    Parameters
    ----------
    config : pytest config
    items : list of collected items
    """
    prev_test_result = {}
    if config.getoption("--skip-passed"):
        cache = config.cache
        prev_test_result = cache.get("cache/lasttestresult", {})

    for item in items:
        if prev_test_result.get(item.nodeid) in ("passed", "warnings", "skip_passed"):
            item.add_marker(pytest.mark.skip(reason="previously passed"))
            continue

        maybe_skip_test(item, delayed=True)


# Save test results to a cache
# Code adapted from: https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/blob/main/src/_pytest/pastebin.py
@pytest.hookimpl(trylast=True)
def pytest_terminal_summary(terminalreporter):
    tr = terminalreporter
    cache = tr.config.cache
    assert cache

    test_result = {}
    for status in tr.stats:
        if status in ("warnings", "deselected"):
            continue

        for test in tr.stats[status]:
            if test.when != "call":  # discard results from setup/teardown
                continue

            try:
                if test.longrepr and test.longrepr[2] in "previously passed":
                    test_result[test.nodeid] = "skip_passed"
                else:
                    test_result[test.nodeid] = test.outcome
            except Exception:
                pass

    cache.set("cache/lasttestresult", test_result)


@pytest.hookimpl(wrapper=True)
def pytest_runtest_call(item):
    """We want to run extra verification at the start and end of each test to
    check that we haven't leaked memory. According to pytest issue #5044, it's
    not possible to "Fail" a test from a fixture (no matter what you do, pytest
    sets the test status to "Error"). The approach suggested there is hook
    pytest_runtest_call as we do here. To get access to the selenium fixture, we
    imitate the definition of pytest_pyfunc_call:
    https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/blob/6.2.2/src/_pytest/python.py#L177

    Pytest issue #5044:
    https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/5044
    """
    browser = None
    for fixture in item._fixtureinfo.argnames:
        if fixture.startswith("selenium"):
            browser = item.funcargs[fixture]
            break

    if not browser or not browser.pyodide_loaded:
        result = yield
        return result

    trace_pyproxies = pytest.mark.skip_pyproxy_check.mark not in item.own_markers
    trace_hiwire_refs = (
        trace_pyproxies and pytest.mark.skip_refcount_check.mark not in item.own_markers
    )
    yield from extra_checks_test_wrapper(
        browser, trace_hiwire_refs, trace_pyproxies, item
    )


def extra_checks_test_wrapper(browser, trace_hiwire_refs, trace_pyproxies, item):
    """Extra conditions for test to pass:
    1. No explicit request for test to fail
    2. No leaked JsRefs
    3. No leaked PyProxys
    """
    browser.clear_force_test_fail()
    init_num_keys = browser.get_num_hiwire_keys()
    if trace_pyproxies:
        browser.enable_pyproxy_tracing()
        init_num_proxies = browser.get_num_proxies()
    err = False
    try:
        result = yield
    except Exception:
        err = True
        raise
    finally:
        # Suppress any errors if an error was raised so we keep the original error
        with contextlib.suppress(Exception) if err else contextlib.nullcontext():
            browser.disable_pyproxy_tracing()
            browser.restore_state()
            # The method_call_singleton holds onto the last called JS method,
            # clear it so we don't fail refcount check
            browser.run_js("pyodide._module._clear_method_call_singleton();")

    if browser.force_test_fail:
        raise Exception("Test failure explicitly requested but no error was raised.")
    if trace_pyproxies and trace_hiwire_refs:
        delta_proxies = browser.get_num_proxies() - init_num_proxies
        delta_keys = browser.get_num_hiwire_keys() - init_num_keys
        if delta_proxies > 0:
            pxs = browser.run_js(
                """
                return Array.from(pyodide._module.pyproxy_alloc_map.entries(), ([x, s]) => [x.type, x.toString(), "Traceback at creation:" + s.replace("Error", "")])
                """
            )
            capman = item.config.pluginmanager.getplugin("capturemanager")
            with capman.item_capture("call", item):
                print("\n" + "!" * 40)
                print("leaked proxies:")
                for row in pxs:
                    print(*row)

        assert (delta_proxies, delta_keys) == (0, 0) or delta_keys < 0
    if trace_hiwire_refs:
        delta_keys = browser.get_num_hiwire_keys() - init_num_keys
        assert delta_keys <= 0
    return result


def package_is_built(package_name):
    return _package_is_built(package_name, pytest.pyodide_dist_dir)  # type:ignore[attr-defined]


def strip_assertions_stderr(messages: Sequence[str]) -> list[str]:
    """Strip additional messages on stderr included when ASSERTIONS=1"""
    res = []
    for msg in messages:
        if msg.strip() in [
            "sigaction: signal type not supported: this is a no-op.",
            "Calling stub instead of siginterrupt()",
            "warning: no blob constructor, cannot create blobs with mimetypes",
            "warning: no BlobBuilder",
        ]:
            continue
        res.append(msg)
    return res
summarization.summary:
### File Overview
`conftest.py` defines global pytest configuration, hooks, and helper utilities used across the test suite, especially for running Pyodide-based tests in different runtimes (chrome/node/safari) and enforcing leak checks.

### Architecture
- **Global configuration bootstrapping**: `set_configs()` is executed at import time to configure `pytest_pyodide` (browser/node flags, initialization scripts, and Pyodide load scripts).
- **Pytest hooks**: Implements multiple pytest hooks (`pytest_addoption`, `pytest_configure`, `pytest_collection_modifyitems`, `pytest_terminal_summary`, `pytest_runtest_call`) to:
  - add CLI options,
  - adjust output formatting,
  - skip tests based on prior results or missing built packages,
  - cache results to speed subsequent runs,
  - wrap each test call with additional leak/health checks when running under selenium/pyodide.
- **Utilities**: Helpers like `package_is_built()` and `strip_assertions_stderr()` support package availability logic and stable stderr assertions.

### Functions & Classes
- `set_configs` — Centralizes configuration of `pytest_pyodide` via `get_global_config()`. Sets:
  - chrome flags (enables `WebAssemblyExperimentalJSPI` but intentionally avoids type reflection),
  - node flags (`--experimental-wasm-stack-switching`),
  - an initialization script that “touches” various Pyodide APIs to avoid refcount-check noise,
  - per-runtime Pyodide loading scripts for `"chrome"` and `"node"` (node uses `snapshot.bin`).
  This is critical for consistent runtime behavior and test stability.

- `pytest_addoption` — Adds two CLI options:
  - `--run-xfail` to actually run xfail-marked tests,
  - `--skip-passed` to skip tests that passed in the previous run (with a warning that it ignores code changes).
  Important for controlling execution and test iteration speed.

- `maybe_skip_test` — Implements fixture-level skipping to avoid expensive selenium startup when a test should be skipped anyway. Key behavior:
  - Detects package tests via path regex `.../packages/<name>/test_*.py`,
  - Skips tests (only for parametrizations that include a browser marker in the node id) when `package_is_built(package_name)` is false,
  - Supports `delayed=True` mode to add a skip marker instead of immediately calling `pytest.skip`.
  This is the main “package index / package availability” gate.

- `pytest_configure` — Monkey-patches `config.cwd_relative_nodeid` to shorten names in pytest’s summary table (removes `src/tests/`, `packages/`, and trims `::test_` to `::`). Also stores `--dist-dir` into `pytest.pyodide_dist_dir` for later use by `package_is_built`.
  Relevant to terminal output readability and locating the dist directory used for package build checks.

- `pytest_collection_modifyitems` — After collection, optionally skips previously passed tests by reading `cache/lasttestresult` when `--skip-passed` is enabled. Also calls `maybe_skip_test(item, delayed=True)` for each item to mark skips without triggering expensive fixtures during collection.
  Important to how package-related tests are filtered before execution.

- `pytest_terminal_summary` — `trylast=True` hook that records per-test outcomes into pytest’s cache key `cache/lasttestresult`. It:
  - ignores `warnings` and `deselected`,
  - only records results from the `"call"` phase (not setup/teardown),
  - attempts to detect the special “previously passed” skip and store `"skip_passed"`.
  This hook directly affects terminal-summary-time behavior and powers `--skip-passed`.

- `pytest_runtest_call` — `wrapper=True` hook that wraps the actual test call to enforce extra verification (memory/leak checks) for tests that use a selenium fixture and have `browser.pyodide_loaded`. It determines whether to trace:
  - PyProxy leaks (skipped by `pytest.mark.skip_pyproxy_check`),
  - hiwire refcount leaks (skipped by `pytest.mark.skip_refcount_check`).
  Then delegates to `extra_checks_test_wrapper(...)`.
  Important for understanding failures that show up as terminal output assertions due to leak diagnostics printing.

- `extra_checks_test_wrapper` — Generator wrapper implementing “extra conditions”:
  1. clears forced-fail flag, snapshots initial hiwire key count and proxy count (if tracing),
  2. yields to run the test,
  3. in `finally`, restores state, disables tracing, and clears `pyodide._module._clear_method_call_singleton()` to avoid false positives,
  4. if proxies leaked, prints a “leaked proxies” section (using pytest capture manager to emit output tied to the test call phase),
  5. asserts no proxy/key leaks (with special allowance `delta_keys < 0` in one combined assertion path).
  This is highly relevant to terminal output/capture behavior because it can print additional diagnostics that tests might need to account for.

- `package_is_built` — Wrapper around `pytest_pyodide.utils.package_is_built` (imported as `_package_is_built`) that supplies `pytest.pyodide_dist_dir`. Used by `maybe_skip_test` to decide whether to skip package tests.
  Relevant to “package index” style tests because it gates whether package tests run at all.

- `strip_assertions_stderr` — Filters out known noisy stderr lines that appear when `ASSERTIONS=1` (e.g., “sigaction…no-op”, blob warnings). Returns a cleaned list of messages.
  Directly relevant to terminal/stderr output assertions: tests can use this to make output comparisons stable across builds.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- For **package index / package availability tests**, `maybe_skip_test` and `package_is_built` are the key mechanisms that skip package tests when artifacts aren’t present in `pytest.pyodide_dist_dir` (set in `pytest_configure`).
- For **terminal output assertions**, the most relevant pieces are:
  - `strip_assertions_stderr`, which normalizes stderr to avoid assertion failures caused by assertion-enabled builds,
  - `pytest_terminal_summary`, which modifies caching behavior based on terminal summary stats,
  - `extra_checks_test_wrapper`, which can print leak diagnostics during the call phase and therefore affect captured output or golden-output comparisons.

from conftest import DIST_PATH, PYODIDE_ROOT


def get_pyparsing_wheel_name() -> str:
    return list(DIST_PATH.glob("pyparsing*.whl"))[0].name


def get_pytz_wheel_name() -> str:
    return list(DIST_PATH.glob("pytz*.whl"))[0].name


@pytest.mark.xfail_browsers(node="Loading urls in node seems to time out right now")
@pytest.mark.parametrize("active_server", ["main", "secondary"])
def test_load_from_url(selenium_standalone, web_server_secondary, active_server):
    selenium = selenium_standalone
    if active_server == "secondary":
        url, port, log_main = web_server_secondary
        log_backup = selenium.server_log
    elif active_server == "main":
        _, _, log_backup = web_server_secondary
        log_main = selenium.server_log
        url = selenium.server_hostname
        port = selenium.server_port
    else:
        raise AssertionError()

    with log_backup.open("r") as fh_backup, log_main.open("r") as fh_main:
        # skip existing log lines
        fh_main.seek(0, 2)
        fh_backup.seek(0, 2)

        pyparsing_wheel_name = get_pyparsing_wheel_name()
        selenium.load_package(f"http://{url}:{port}/{pyparsing_wheel_name}")
        assert "Skipping unknown package" not in selenium.logs
        assert "Loading pyparsing" in selenium.logs
        assert "Loaded pyparsing" in selenium.logs

        # check that all resources were loaded from the active server
        txt = fh_main.read()
        assert f'"GET /{pyparsing_wheel_name} HTTP/1.1" 200' in txt

        # no additional resources were loaded from the other server
        assert len(fh_backup.read()) == 0

    selenium.run(
        """
        from pyparsing import Word, alphas
        repr(Word(alphas).parseString('hello'))
        """
    )

    pytz_wheel_name = get_pytz_wheel_name()
    selenium.load_package(f"http://{url}:{port}/{pytz_wheel_name}")
    selenium.run("import pytz")

def test_get_dynlibs():
    import tarfile
    from tempfile import NamedTemporaryFile
    from zipfile import ZipFile

    from pyodide._package_loader import get_dynlibs

    files = [
        "a",
        "a.so",
        "a.py",
        "a.txt",
        "a/b.so",
        "a/b.txt",
        "a/b.py",
        "b/a.py",
        "b/b.so",
        "a/b/c/d.so",
    ]
    so_files = sorted("/p/" + f for f in files if f.endswith(".so"))
    with NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=".bz") as t:
        x = tarfile.open(mode="x:bz2", fileobj=t)
        for file in files:
            x.addfile(tarfile.TarInfo(file))
        x.close()
        t.flush()
        assert sorted(get_dynlibs(t, ".bz", Path("/p"))) == so_files
    with NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=".zip") as t:
        x2 = ZipFile(t, mode="w")
        for file in files:
            x2.writestr(file, "")
        x2.close()
        t.flush()
        assert sorted(get_dynlibs(t, ".zip", Path("/p"))) == so_files

class DummyDistribution:
    def __init__(
        self,
        name: str,
        source: str | None = None,
        direct_url: dict[str, str] | None = None,
        installer: str | None = None,
        version: str = "0.0.1",
    ):
        self.name = name
        self.version = version
        direct_url_json = json.dumps(direct_url) if direct_url else None
        self._files: dict[str, str | None] = {
            "PYODIDE_SOURCE": source,
            "direct_url.json": direct_url_json,
            "INSTALLER": installer,
        }

    @property
    def dist_info_name(self):
        # https://packaging.python.org/en/latest/specifications/name-normalization/#normalization
        normalized_name = re.sub(r"[-_.]+", "-", self.name).lower()
        return f"{normalized_name}-{self.version}.dist-info"

    def write(self, base_dir: Path) -> None:
        dist_info_dir = base_dir / self.dist_info_name
        dist_info_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
        for key, value in self._files.items():
            if value is not None:
                (dist_info_dir / key).write_text(value)
        with (dist_info_dir / "METADATA").open("w") as f:
            # fmt: off
            f.write(
                "Metadata-Version: 2.1\n"
                f"Name: {self.name}\n"
                f"Version: {self.version}\n"
            )
            # fmt: on

    def __repr__(self):
        return self.name

### File Overview
`src/tests/test_package_loading.py` is a pytest suite covering Pyodide package loading/install behavior from names and URLs (wheels), loaded package bookkeeping (`pyodide.loadedPackages`), and several lower-level helpers in `pyodide._package_loader` (dynlib detection, wheel metadata parsing, dist source detection). It includes multiple tests that assert on log/terminal output, making it a key place for brittle stdout/stderr expectations.

### Architecture
- **High-level integration tests (browser/pyodide runtime):** Most tests use `selenium` fixtures from `pytest_pyodide` to drive a Pyodide instance and call `pyodide.loadPackage(...)` / `selenium.load_package(...)`, then verify runtime behavior via `selenium.run(...)` and/or `selenium.run_js(...)`. Many assertions inspect `selenium.logs`, which aggregates console/log output from the JS side.
- **Web-server based URL loading tests:** Some tests spin up primary/secondary servers (`web_server_main`, `web_server_secondary`, `spawn_web_server`) to validate where resources are fetched from and to ensure no accidental cross-server loads.
- **Pure-Python unit tests for `_package_loader`:** Several tests import functions from `pyodide._package_loader` and validate behavior without selenium (e.g., dynlib filename detection, parsing wheel structures, dist-info source attribution).
- **Test utilities:** `get_pyparsing_wheel_name()` and `get_pytz_wheel_name()` locate wheel artifacts under `DIST_PATH`. `DummyDistribution` is used to synthesize `.dist-info` directories for source/installer provenance tests.

### Functions & Classes
- `get_pyparsing_wheel_name` — Finds the first wheel in `DIST_PATH` matching `pyparsing*.whl` and returns its filename. Important for URL-based package loading tests that need an actual wheel filename.
- `get_pytz_wheel_name` — Same as above, but for `pytz*.whl`. Used in relative-URL and general loading tests.

- `test_load_from_url` — Integration test that loads a wheel from an explicit `http://{host}:{port}/{wheel}` URL, asserts log messages (e.g. “Loading pyparsing”, “Loaded pyparsing”), and inspects server logs to ensure the active server served the wheel and the inactive server served nothing. **Highly relevant for brittle output/log assertions and package index/URL loading behavior.**
- `test_load_relative_url` — Builds a temporary HTML page pointing at `pyodide.js` from the main server, hosts a temp directory containing a wheel, then loads that wheel via a *relative path* (and in node, changes cwd). Verifies that `loadedPackages.pytz` is printed. Relevant to path resolution and environment differences (node vs browsers) and any output/log checking around loading.
- `test_list_loaded_urls` — Loads `pyparsing` by name and asserts `Object.keys(pyodide.loadedPackages)` and that `pyodide.loadedPackages['pyparsing'] == "default channel"`. Relevant to how Pyodide records “source” strings for loaded packages.
- `test_uri_mismatch` — Loads `pyparsing` by name, then tries to load a wheel URL for a specific pyparsing wheel; asserts a warning-like log substring: `"URI mismatch, attempting to load package pyparsing"`. **Directly relevant to terminal/log output brittleness.**
- `test_invalid_package_name` — Ensures invalid names/URLs raise `selenium.JavascriptException` with specific `match=` regex/messages. Relevant to validation error strings (brittle if error text changes).
- `test_load_package_return` — Calls `pyodide.loadPackage('pyparsing')` via JS and asserts returned metadata fields (`name`, `packageType`). Relevant to the JS API return contract.
- `test_load_package_return_from_url` — Same as above but for URL-based wheel loading; asserts `fileName` equals the wheel filename. Relevant to return schema and URL-loading path.
- `test_load_packages_multiple` — Calls `selenium.load_package(packages)` with two packages at once and asserts the combined “Loaded a, b” log line occurs exactly once (either order). **This is a brittle log-format/order assertion and is important for PRs changing console output.**
- `test_load_packages_sequential` — Uses `Promise.all([pyodide.loadPackage(...), ...])` to load sequentially/parallel, asserts per-package “Loaded X” appears exactly once, and that returned list of names matches either order. Relevant to deduping logic and log emission count.
- `test_load_handle_failure` — Loads `pytz`, then tries (and expects failure) loading `pytz2`, then loads `pyparsing`; asserts both successful load log lines exist. Relevant to ensuring failures don’t poison subsequent loads and log continuity.
- `test_load_failure_retry` — Loads from an invalid URL to force a failure; asserts exact counts of log lines: `"Loading pytz"` and `"The following error occurred while loading pytz:"`, then ensures `pyodide.loadedPackages` is empty; subsequently loads `pytz` successfully. **Very sensitive to error message text and logging counts.**
- `test_load_twice` — Loads `pytz` twice and asserts `"No new packages to load"` is in logs. Relevant to dedupe messaging (brittle string).
- `test_load_twice_different_source` — Loads two different URLs for the same wheel and asserts an exact long log message about “Loading same package pytz from … and …”. **Highly brittle exact-string assertion.**
- `test_load_twice_same_source` — Loads the same URL twice and asserts the “Loading same package pytz” warning is *not* in logs. Relevant to duplicate-source suppression logic.
- `test_js_load_package_from_python` — Runs Python code via `pyodide.runPythonAsync` that imports `loadPackage` from `pyodide_js` and awaits it; then asserts `"Loaded pyparsing"` appears and `pyodide.loadedPackages` contains it. Relevant to Python<->JS bridge API and log output.
- `test_load_package_mixed_case` — Parameterized over `"jinja2"` and `"Jinja2"`; ensures `pyodide.loadPackage` works regardless of case and import succeeds. Relevant to name normalization.
- `test_test_unvendoring` — (Requires dynamic linking) Loads `regex` then checks that its tests are not present; loads `regex-tests` and verifies they appear; finally asserts `pyodide._api.lockfile_packages['regex'].unvendored_tests` is truthy. Relevant to lockfile metadata and package splitting behavior.
- `test_install_archive` — Uses `shutil.make_archive` to create `test_pkg.tar.gz`, then tests `pyodide.unpackArchive` with multiple format strings, imports the unpacked package, runs assertions, then cleans up. Relevant to archive format handling and unpack/install behavior (less about package index logs).
- `test_load_bad_so_file` — (Requires dynamic linking) Writes a fake `/a.so`, calls `pyodide._api.loadDynlib`, and asserts a specific warning string about failing to load a dynlib. **Brittle log string assertion.**
- `test_should_load_dynlib` — Unit test for `pyodide._package_loader.should_load_dynlib`, verifying which filenames should/should not be treated as dynlibs based on suffix patterns and `EXT_SUFFIX`. Relevant to loader heuristics.
- `test_get_dynlibs` — Unit test for `pyodide._package_loader.get_dynlibs` against both tar and zip archives, ensuring `.so` paths are detected and prefixed with a base path. Relevant to archive scanning logic.
- `test_find_wheel_metadata_dir` — Unit test for `pyodide._package_loader.find_wheel_metadata_dir`, verifying it finds directories containing `METADATA` by suffix and returns `None` if absent. Relevant to wheel introspection.
- `test_wheel_dist_info_dir` — Unit test for `pyodide._package_loader.wheel_dist_info_dir` and `UnsupportedWheel`, verifying correct `.dist-info` selection and expected exceptions/messages. Relevant to error messaging and dist-info name normalization.
- `test_wheel_data_file_dir` — Unit test for `pyodide._package_loader.wheel_data_file_dir`, verifying discovery of `{pkg}.data` directories and name normalization behavior. Relevant to data-file installation support.

- `DummyDistribution` — Helper class to synthesize an installed distribution’s `.dist-info` directory with optional `PYODIDE_SOURCE`, `direct_url.json`, and `INSTALLER` files plus basic `METADATA`.
  - `__init__` — Captures distribution attributes and prepares a `_files` mapping for optional dist-info files.
  - `dist_info_name` (property) — Produces normalized `{name}-{version}.dist-info` using PEP 503-style normalization (`re.sub(r"[-_.]+", "-", ...)`). Important for matching how package metadata directories are named.
  - `write` — Creates the dist-info directory under `base_dir` and writes the optional files and `METADATA`. Core to tests that validate `get_dist_source` and `init_loaded_packages`.
  - `__repr__` — Returns `self.name` for readable parametrized test output.

- `test_get_dist_source` — Parameterized over `result_dist_pairs`; imports `pyodide._package_loader.get_dist_source`, writes a dummy dist, and asserts the returned `(dist.name, result)` matches expectation. Relevant to how `loadedPackages` source strings are computed (default channel vs URL vs installer unknown).
- `test_init_loaded_packages` — Monkeypatches `_package_loader.SITE_PACKAGES` and `_package_loader.loadedPackages`, writes many dummy dists, calls `_package_loader.init_loaded_packages()`, then asserts attributes exist on `loadedPackages` with the expected source string. Relevant to initialization and the shape of `loadedPackages` exposed to JS.
- `test_custom_lockfile` — (xfail node, requires dynamic linking) Creates a lockfile via `micropip.freeze()`, writes it as `custom_lockfile.json`, then loads Pyodide with `lockFileURL` and asserts importing `hypothesis` yields version `6.47.3`. Relevant to lockfile/package index mechanics (less to stdout/stderr, more to behavior).
- `test_normalized_name` — Parameterized name normalization cases; uses `messageCallback` to collect messages from `pyodide.loadPackage`, then asserts “Loading …” and “Loaded …” messages include the *real* package name and that `pyodide.loadedPackages` includes it. **Very relevant to log message formats and name normalization; message callback makes this especially sensitive to output changes.**
- `test_data_files_support` — Serves a `dummy_pkg` wheel via `httpserver`, loads it by URL, then (via `@run_in_pyodide`) asserts installed data files exist under `sys.prefix/share` and `sys.prefix/etc`. Relevant to wheel `.data` handling.
- `test_install_api` — Fetches the same `dummy_pkg` wheel and directly calls `pyodide._api.install(...)` into a specified install dir with extra metadata (`INSTALLER=pytest`), then verifies dist-info and package directories exist via `@run_in_pyodide`. Relevant to low-level installation API behavior and metadata file writing.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
This file is one of the primary locations where package loading (by index name and by URL) is tested and where **terminal/log output is asserted**. The most brittle assertions to adjust for output changes are in:
- `test_load_from_url` (expects exact “Loading …” / “Loaded …” substrings and server log behavior),
- `test_uri_mismatch` (expects a specific warning substring),
- `test_load_packages_multiple` / `test_load_packages_sequential` (expects specific “Loaded …” message formats and counts),
- `test_load_failure_retry` (expects exact error log prefix and counts),
- `test_load_twice` / `test_load_twice_different_source` (expects exact dedupe/warning strings),
- `test_load_bad_so_file` (expects an exact warning message),
- `test_normalized_name` (expects messages collected by `messageCallback` to start with “Loading”/“Loaded” and include the real package name).

If the PR changes how Pyodide/micropip reports progress, warning text, ordering of “Loaded …” messages, or the source strings stored in `pyodide.loadedPackages`, this suite is likely to fail and is the right place to relax string matching, adjust regexes, or update expected messages.

    if active_server == "secondary":
        url, port, log_main = web_server_secondary
        log_backup = selenium.server_log
    elif active_server == "main":
        _, _, log_backup = web_server_secondary
        log_main = selenium.server_log
        url = selenium.server_hostname
        port = selenium.server_port
    else:
        raise AssertionError()

    with log_backup.open("r") as fh_backup, log_main.open("r") as fh_main:
        # skip existing log lines
        fh_main.seek(0, 2)
        fh_backup.seek(0, 2)

        pyparsing_wheel_name = get_pyparsing_wheel_name()
        selenium.load_package(f"http://{url}:{port}/{pyparsing_wheel_name}")
        assert "Skipping unknown package" not in selenium.logs
        assert "Loading pyparsing" in selenium.logs
        assert "Loaded pyparsing" in selenium.logs

        # check that all resources were loaded from the active server
        txt = fh_main.read()
        assert f'"GET /{pyparsing_wheel_name} HTTP/1.1" 200' in txt

        # no additional resources were loaded from the other server
        assert len(fh_backup.read()) == 0

    selenium.run(
        """
        from pyparsing import Word, alphas
        repr(Word(alphas).parseString('hello'))
        """
    )

    pytz_wheel_name = get_pytz_wheel_name()
    selenium.load_package(f"http://{url}:{port}/{pytz_wheel_name}")
    selenium.run("import pytz")

def test_should_load_dynlib():
    import sysconfig

    from pyodide._package_loader import should_load_dynlib

    ext_suffix = sysconfig.get_config_var("EXT_SUFFIX")
    assert ext_suffix
    should_load = [
        "a.so",

def test_get_dynlibs():
    import tarfile
    from tempfile import NamedTemporaryFile
    from zipfile import ZipFile

    from pyodide._package_loader import get_dynlibs

    files = [
        "a",
        "a.so",
        "a.py",
        "a.txt",
        "a/b.so",
        "a/b.txt",
        "a/b.py",
        "b/a.py",
        "b/b.so",
        "a/b/c/d.so",
    ]
    so_files = sorted("/p/" + f for f in files if f.endswith(".so"))
    with NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=".bz") as t:
        x = tarfile.open(mode="x:bz2", fileobj=t)
        for file in files:
            x.addfile(tarfile.TarInfo(file))
        x.close()
        t.flush()
        assert sorted(get_dynlibs(t, ".bz", Path("/p"))) == so_files
    with NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=".zip") as t:
        x2 = ZipFile(t, mode="w")
        for file in files:
            x2.writestr(file, "")
        x2.close()
        t.flush()
        assert sorted(get_dynlibs(t, ".zip", Path("/p"))) == so_files

class DummyDistribution:
    def __init__(
        self,
        name: str,
        source: str | None = None,
        direct_url: dict[str, str] | None = None,
        installer: str | None = None,
        version: str = "0.0.1",
    ):
        self.name = name
        self.version = version
        direct_url_json = json.dumps(direct_url) if direct_url else None
        self._files: dict[str, str | None] = {
            "PYODIDE_SOURCE": source,
            "direct_url.json": direct_url_json,
            "INSTALLER": installer,
        }

    @property
    def dist_info_name(self):
        # https://packaging.python.org/en/latest/specifications/name-normalization/#normalization
        normalized_name = re.sub(r"[-_.]+", "-", self.name).lower()
        return f"{normalized_name}-{self.version}.dist-info"

    def write(self, base_dir: Path) -> None:
        dist_info_dir = base_dir / self.dist_info_name
        dist_info_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
        for key, value in self._files.items():
            if value is not None:
                (dist_info_dir / key).write_text(value)
        with (dist_info_dir / "METADATA").open("w") as f:
            # fmt: off
            f.write(
                "Metadata-Version: 2.1\n"
                f"Name: {self.name}\n"
                f"Version: {self.version}\n"
            )
            # fmt: on

    def __repr__(self):
        return self.name

    selenium = selenium_standalone

    httpserver.expect_oneshot_request("/" + test_file_name).respond_with_data(
        test_file_data,
        content_type="application/zip",
        headers={"Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*"},
        status=200,
    )
    request_url = httpserver.url_for("/" + test_file_name)

    selenium.run_js(
        f"""
        await pyodide.loadPackage("{request_url}");
        """
    )

    @run_in_pyodide
    def _run(selenium):

def test_install_api(selenium_standalone, httpserver):
    selenium = selenium_standalone

    test_file_name = "dummy_pkg-0.1.0-py3-none-any.whl"
    test_file_path = Path(__file__).parent / "wheels" / test_file_name
    test_file_data = test_file_path.read_bytes()
    install_dir = "/random_install_dir"

    httpserver.expect_oneshot_request("/" + test_file_name).respond_with_data(
        test_file_data,
        content_type="application/zip",
        headers={"Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*"},
        status=200,
    )
    request_url = httpserver.url_for("/" + test_file_name)

    selenium.run_js(
        f"""
        wheelData = await fetch("{request_url}");
        wheelDataArr = new Uint8Array(await wheelData.arrayBuffer());
        await pyodide._api.install(
          wheelDataArr,
          "{test_file_name}",
          "{install_dir}",
          new Map([["INSTALLER", "pytest"]])
        );
        """
    )

    @run_in_pyodide
    def _run(selenium, pkg_dir):
        import pathlib

        d = pathlib.Path(pkg_dir)
        assert d.is_dir(), f"Directory {d} not found"
        assert (d / "dummy_pkg-0.1.0.dist-info").is_dir(), (
            "dist-info directory not found"
        )
        assert (d / "dummy_pkg-0.1.0.dist-info" / "INSTALLER").is_file(), (
            "INSTALLER file not found"
        )
        assert (d / "dummy_pkg").is_dir(), "package directory not found"

    _run(selenium, install_dir)
summarization.summary:
### File Overview
`src/tests/test_package_loading.py` is a pytest suite covering Pyodide package loading/install behavior from names and URLs (wheels), loaded package bookkeeping (`pyodide.loadedPackages`), and several lower-level helpers in `pyodide._package_loader` (dynlib detection, wheel metadata parsing, dist source detection). It includes multiple tests that assert on log/terminal output, making it a key place for brittle stdout/stderr expectations.

### Architecture
- **High-level integration tests (browser/pyodide runtime):** Most tests use `selenium` fixtures from `pytest_pyodide` to drive a Pyodide instance and call `pyodide.loadPackage(...)` / `selenium.load_package(...)`, then verify runtime behavior via `selenium.run(...)` and/or `selenium.run_js(...)`. Many assertions inspect `selenium.logs`, which aggregates console/log output from the JS side.
- **Web-server based URL loading tests:** Some tests spin up primary/secondary servers (`web_server_main`, `web_server_secondary`, `spawn_web_server`) to validate where resources are fetched from and to ensure no accidental cross-server loads.
- **Pure-Python unit tests for `_package_loader`:** Several tests import functions from `pyodide._package_loader` and validate behavior without selenium (e.g., dynlib filename detection, parsing wheel structures, dist-info source attribution).
- **Test utilities:** `get_pyparsing_wheel_name()` and `get_pytz_wheel_name()` locate wheel artifacts under `DIST_PATH`. `DummyDistribution` is used to synthesize `.dist-info` directories for source/installer provenance tests.

### Functions & Classes
- `get_pyparsing_wheel_name` — Finds the first wheel in `DIST_PATH` matching `pyparsing*.whl` and returns its filename. Important for URL-based package loading tests that need an actual wheel filename.
- `get_pytz_wheel_name` — Same as above, but for `pytz*.whl`. Used in relative-URL and general loading tests.

- `test_load_from_url` — Integration test that loads a wheel from an explicit `http://{host}:{port}/{wheel}` URL, asserts log messages (e.g. “Loading pyparsing”, “Loaded pyparsing”), and inspects server logs to ensure the active server served the wheel and the inactive server served nothing. **Highly relevant for brittle output/log assertions and package index/URL loading behavior.**
- `test_load_relative_url` — Builds a temporary HTML page pointing at `pyodide.js` from the main server, hosts a temp directory containing a wheel, then loads that wheel via a *relative path* (and in node, changes cwd). Verifies that `loadedPackages.pytz` is printed. Relevant to path resolution and environment differences (node vs browsers) and any output/log checking around loading.
- `test_list_loaded_urls` — Loads `pyparsing` by name and asserts `Object.keys(pyodide.loadedPackages)` and that `pyodide.loadedPackages['pyparsing'] == "default channel"`. Relevant to how Pyodide records “source” strings for loaded packages.
- `test_uri_mismatch` — Loads `pyparsing` by name, then tries to load a wheel URL for a specific pyparsing wheel; asserts a warning-like log substring: `"URI mismatch, attempting to load package pyparsing"`. **Directly relevant to terminal/log output brittleness.**
- `test_invalid_package_name` — Ensures invalid names/URLs raise `selenium.JavascriptException` with specific `match=` regex/messages. Relevant to validation error strings (brittle if error text changes).
- `test_load_package_return` — Calls `pyodide.loadPackage('pyparsing')` via JS and asserts returned metadata fields (`name`, `packageType`). Relevant to the JS API return contract.
- `test_load_package_return_from_url` — Same as above but for URL-based wheel loading; asserts `fileName` equals the wheel filename. Relevant to return schema and URL-loading path.
- `test_load_packages_multiple` — Calls `selenium.load_package(packages)` with two packages at once and asserts the combined “Loaded a, b” log line occurs exactly once (either order). **This is a brittle log-format/order assertion and is important for PRs changing console output.**
- `test_load_packages_sequential` — Uses `Promise.all([pyodide.loadPackage(...), ...])` to load sequentially/parallel, asserts per-package “Loaded X” appears exactly once, and that returned list of names matches either order. Relevant to deduping logic and log emission count.
- `test_load_handle_failure` — Loads `pytz`, then tries (and expects failure) loading `pytz2`, then loads `pyparsing`; asserts both successful load log lines exist. Relevant to ensuring failures don’t poison subsequent loads and log continuity.
- `test_load_failure_retry` — Loads from an invalid URL to force a failure; asserts exact counts of log lines: `"Loading pytz"` and `"The following error occurred while loading pytz:"`, then ensures `pyodide.loadedPackages` is empty; subsequently loads `pytz` successfully. **Very sensitive to error message text and logging counts.**
- `test_load_twice` — Loads `pytz` twice and asserts `"No new packages to load"` is in logs. Relevant to dedupe messaging (brittle string).
- `test_load_twice_different_source` — Loads two different URLs for the same wheel and asserts an exact long log message about “Loading same package pytz from … and …”. **Highly brittle exact-string assertion.**
- `test_load_twice_same_source` — Loads the same URL twice and asserts the “Loading same package pytz” warning is *not* in logs. Relevant to duplicate-source suppression logic.
- `test_js_load_package_from_python` — Runs Python code via `pyodide.runPythonAsync` that imports `loadPackage` from `pyodide_js` and awaits it; then asserts `"Loaded pyparsing"` appears and `pyodide.loadedPackages` contains it. Relevant to Python<->JS bridge API and log output.
- `test_load_package_mixed_case` — Parameterized over `"jinja2"` and `"Jinja2"`; ensures `pyodide.loadPackage` works regardless of case and import succeeds. Relevant to name normalization.
- `test_test_unvendoring` — (Requires dynamic linking) Loads `regex` then checks that its tests are not present; loads `regex-tests` and verifies they appear; finally asserts `pyodide._api.lockfile_packages['regex'].unvendored_tests` is truthy. Relevant to lockfile metadata and package splitting behavior.
- `test_install_archive` — Uses `shutil.make_archive` to create `test_pkg.tar.gz`, then tests `pyodide.unpackArchive` with multiple format strings, imports the unpacked package, runs assertions, then cleans up. Relevant to archive format handling and unpack/install behavior (less about package index logs).
- `test_load_bad_so_file` — (Requires dynamic linking) Writes a fake `/a.so`, calls `pyodide._api.loadDynlib`, and asserts a specific warning string about failing to load a dynlib. **Brittle log string assertion.**
- `test_should_load_dynlib` — Unit test for `pyodide._package_loader.should_load_dynlib`, verifying which filenames should/should not be treated as dynlibs based on suffix patterns and `EXT_SUFFIX`. Relevant to loader heuristics.
- `test_get_dynlibs` — Unit test for `pyodide._package_loader.get_dynlibs` against both tar and zip archives, ensuring `.so` paths are detected and prefixed with a base path. Relevant to archive scanning logic.
- `test_find_wheel_metadata_dir` — Unit test for `pyodide._package_loader.find_wheel_metadata_dir`, verifying it finds directories containing `METADATA` by suffix and returns `None` if absent. Relevant to wheel introspection.
- `test_wheel_dist_info_dir` — Unit test for `pyodide._package_loader.wheel_dist_info_dir` and `UnsupportedWheel`, verifying correct `.dist-info` selection and expected exceptions/messages. Relevant to error messaging and dist-info name normalization.
- `test_wheel_data_file_dir` — Unit test for `pyodide._package_loader.wheel_data_file_dir`, verifying discovery of `{pkg}.data` directories and name normalization behavior. Relevant to data-file installation support.

- `DummyDistribution` — Helper class to synthesize an installed distribution’s `.dist-info` directory with optional `PYODIDE_SOURCE`, `direct_url.json`, and `INSTALLER` files plus basic `METADATA`.
  - `__init__` — Captures distribution attributes and prepares a `_files` mapping for optional dist-info files.
  - `dist_info_name` (property) — Produces normalized `{name}-{version}.dist-info` using PEP 503-style normalization (`re.sub(r"[-_.]+", "-", ...)`). Important for matching how package metadata directories are named.
  - `write` — Creates the dist-info directory under `base_dir` and writes the optional files and `METADATA`. Core to tests that validate `get_dist_source` and `init_loaded_packages`.
  - `__repr__` — Returns `self.name` for readable parametrized test output.

- `test_get_dist_source` — Parameterized over `result_dist_pairs`; imports `pyodide._package_loader.get_dist_source`, writes a dummy dist, and asserts the returned `(dist.name, result)` matches expectation. Relevant to how `loadedPackages` source strings are computed (default channel vs URL vs installer unknown).
- `test_init_loaded_packages` — Monkeypatches `_package_loader.SITE_PACKAGES` and `_package_loader.loadedPackages`, writes many dummy dists, calls `_package_loader.init_loaded_packages()`, then asserts attributes exist on `loadedPackages` with the expected source string. Relevant to initialization and the shape of `loadedPackages` exposed to JS.
- `test_custom_lockfile` — (xfail node, requires dynamic linking) Creates a lockfile via `micropip.freeze()`, writes it as `custom_lockfile.json`, then loads Pyodide with `lockFileURL` and asserts importing `hypothesis` yields version `6.47.3`. Relevant to lockfile/package index mechanics (less to stdout/stderr, more to behavior).
- `test_normalized_name` — Parameterized name normalization cases; uses `messageCallback` to collect messages from `pyodide.loadPackage`, then asserts “Loading …” and “Loaded …” messages include the *real* package name and that `pyodide.loadedPackages` includes it. **Very relevant to log message formats and name normalization; message callback makes this especially sensitive to output changes.**
- `test_data_files_support` — Serves a `dummy_pkg` wheel via `httpserver`, loads it by URL, then (via `@run_in_pyodide`) asserts installed data files exist under `sys.prefix/share` and `sys.prefix/etc`. Relevant to wheel `.data` handling.
- `test_install_api` — Fetches the same `dummy_pkg` wheel and directly calls `pyodide._api.install(...)` into a specified install dir with extra metadata (`INSTALLER=pytest`), then verifies dist-info and package directories exist via `@run_in_pyodide`. Relevant to low-level installation API behavior and metadata file writing.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
This file is one of the primary locations where package loading (by index name and by URL) is tested and where **terminal/log output is asserted**. The most brittle assertions to adjust for output changes are in:
- `test_load_from_url` (expects exact “Loading …” / “Loaded …” substrings and server log behavior),
- `test_uri_mismatch` (expects a specific warning substring),
- `test_load_packages_multiple` / `test_load_packages_sequential` (expects specific “Loaded …” message formats and counts),
- `test_load_failure_retry` (expects exact error log prefix and counts),
- `test_load_twice` / `test_load_twice_different_source` (expects exact dedupe/warning strings),
- `test_load_bad_so_file` (expects an exact warning message),
- `test_normalized_name` (expects messages collected by `messageCallback` to start with “Loading”/“Loaded” and include the real package name).

If the PR changes how Pyodide/micropip reports progress, warning text, ordering of “Loaded …” messages, or the source strings stored in `pyodide.loadedPackages`, this suite is likely to fail and is the right place to relax string matching, adjust regexes, or update expected messages.

steps.operation: Extend stderr normalization to filter or collapse newly introduced noisy assertion/runtime lines (while preserving real tracebacks), and ensure tests that compare stderr use this helper consistently.
steps.reason: If the failure is due to extra terminal output on stderr (not just selenium/browser logs), centralizing filtering in the existing helper prevents repeated brittle test changes and keeps stderr comparisons stable across environments/dependency updates.
steps.side_effects: Over-filtering could hide legitimate failures. Implement conservative filtering (target known noise patterns only) and ensure failing tracebacks/errors remain intact; may require updating any tests that rely on the previous exact stderr formatting.

step_plan.summary: Fixes the failing package index/loading tests by making their log/stderr assertions resilient to newly introduced extra terminal output. The change focuses on asserting essential semantic signals (package names, success/failure markers, and source/URI details) and, if needed, filtering known-noisy stderr lines centrally in pytest configuration.
